Brand Prospector
Reuters Advertising · EMEA
How to use Brand Prospector
What this tool is

Brand Prospector shows you which brands are actively spending on branded content with Reuters' competitors — FT, Bloomberg, Economist, WSJ, Forbes, Fortune, Politico Europe and others — so you can identify who to approach, what they're spending on, and how to pitch against the publishers they're already using.

Data source: Contalto competitor intelligence · Jun 2025–Jun 2026 · Web articles only · 1,155 brands tracked

The default view: Hot Prospects

The tool opens on Hot Prospects — EMEA brands in B2B core sectors that have published at least one article on a competitor in the last 90 days, and are not yet Reuters clients. This is your daily outreach list: active budget, right audience, no existing Reuters relationship.

Switch to All brands in the View toggle to see the full 1,155-brand dataset including clients, flagged sectors, and inactive brands.

Filters
${[ ['Search', 'Find a brand by name. Also matches on parent advertiser name.'], ['Geography', 'Filter by where the brand is spending — derived from publisher selection, not the brand\'s HQ country. Europe = FT, Economist, Politico Europe. MENA = Gulf publishers. US = WSJ, Forbes, Bloomberg etc.'], ['Sector', 'Filter to a specific sector. Core B2B sectors are shown in the Hot Prospects default. Flagged sectors (Travel, Luxury, NGOs etc.) are visible when browsing all brands.'], ['Publisher', 'Show only brands spending on a specific competitor publisher.'], ['Recency', 'All time = full 12-month dataset. Active 90d = brands that have published at least one article in the last 90 days.'], ['Reuters relationship', 'Clients = brands already working with Reuters. Prospects = brands not yet on Reuters. All = everything.'], ['View', 'Hot Prospects = pre-set EMEA + core sectors + active 90d + prospects only. All brands = full unfiltered dataset.'], ].map(([label, desc]) => `
${label}
${desc}
`).join('')}
Reading the table
${[ ['✓ Reuters client', 'Orange badge on the brand name — this brand already works with Reuters. Use the Battlecard to build an upsell pitch.'], ['Publisher chips', 'Orange chips = Tier 1 direct competitors (FT, Bloomberg, WSJ etc.). Muted chips = Tier 2 adjacent publishers (BBC, CNBC, Euronews). Colour signals how directly you\'re competing.'], ['Last 90d badge', 'The coloured dot and number — how many articles the brand published in the last 90 days. Hot (orange) = 50+. Warm = 10–49. Cool = 1–9. Cold = none.'], ['↑ Accelerating', 'Brand is publishing significantly more than its own trailing average right now. Strongest "spending now" signal.'], ['↓ Gone quiet', 'Brand had meaningful activity but has published nothing in 60+ days. Prime re-engagement target.'], ['Flagged label', 'Sector is not B2B core (e.g. Travel, Luxury, NGO). Can still be a valid prospect — judge case by case.'], ].map(([label, desc]) => `
${label}
${desc}
`).join('')}
Expanded brand row

Click any row to expand it. You'll see a publisher breakdown bar chart, recent articles (clickable, live links to competitor content), and a summary panel with article count and publishers reached.

The article links let you read what the brand is publishing with competitors — useful for understanding their content strategy before a pitch.

⚡ Battlecard

Click the orange ⚡ Battlecard button on any row to open a pre-built pitch card for that brand. It includes:

  • A pitch angle (upsell for existing clients, diversification for single-publisher brands, gap-fill for multi-publisher)
  • A one-liner to open with in the room
  • Publisher takedowns — specific weaknesses for each publisher the brand is on, with a verbatim "killer line"
  • Reuters advantage stats (trust score, audience reach, sector-specific figures)
  • Objection handlers for the most common pushbacks

Note: Prospect status is not verified against the Salesforce pipeline. A brand showing as a prospect may already be in pitch or have content in production. Confirm before outreaching.

Export

The ↓ Export CSV button downloads your current filtered view as a spreadsheet — brand, sector, geos, article counts, publishers and sample URLs. Useful for sharing a shortlist with your seller or dropping into a call prep doc.
